revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hokotsuki-jinja]][[ja:桙衝神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hokotsuki Shrine | native_name = 桙衝神社 | image = Hokotsuki-jinja, haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hokotsuki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.270931|140.261501}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Hokotsuki Shrine'''|桙衝神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (worship)|lt=Kashima Shrine (worship)|qid=Q125316862|1=Kashima Shrine (worship)}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11658590|ltq=Q11658590|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 82 ** P155: {{ill|Q135040040|ltq=Q135040040|qid=Q135040040|1=Komatsuno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|lt=Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|fr|Isasumi-jinja|ja|伊佐須美神社|zh|伊佐須美神社|qid=Q3155136|1=Isasumi Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q819664|ltq=Q819664|qid=Q819664|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}} * {{ill|Q907389|ltq=Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.270931|140.261501}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Hokotsuki-jinja, haiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182119 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Japan|lt=Japan|ab|Иапониа|ace|Jeupang|ady|Японие|af|Japan|als|Japan|alt|Јопон|ami|Ripon|am|ጃፓን|ang|Iapan|anp|जापान|an|Chapón|arc|ܝܦܢ|ar|اليابان|arnews|تصنيف:اليابان|arquote|اليابان|ary|الجاپون|arz|اليابان|ast|Xapón|as|জাপান|atj|Japan|avk|Nipona|av|Япония|awa|जापान|ay|Nihun|azb|ژاپون|az|Yaponiya|azquote|Yaponiya|ban|Jepang|bar|Japan|bat_smg|Japuonėjė|ba|Япония|bbc|Japang|bcl|Hapon|bdr|Gipun|be_x_old|Японія|be|Японія|bew|Jepang|bg|Япония|bgnews|Категория:Япония|bh|जापान|bi|Japan|bjn|Japang|blk|ဂျပန်ခမ်းထီ|bm|Japan|bn|জাপান|bnquote|জাপান|bnvoyage|জাপান|bo|ཉི་ཧོང་།|bpy|জাপান|br|Japan|bs|Japan|bsnews|Kategorija:Japan|btm|Jepang|bug|ᨍᨛᨄ|bxr|Япон tSeapáin|gcr|Japon|gd|An Iapan|glk|ژاپؤن|gl|Xapón|gn|Hapõ|gom|जपान|gor|Japangi|gorquote|Jepang|got|𐌾𐌰𐍀𐌰𐌽|gu|જાપાન|guw|Japan|gv|Yn Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6380005005208 == Commons category (P373) == * Hokotsuki-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県須賀川市桙衝字亀居山97-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hokotsuki-jinja}} [[JA:桙衝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282636}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Hokotsuki Shrine |Image=[[File:Hokotsuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sukagawa City|Q819664|qid=Q819664|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}} Hokotsuki, Aza Kameiyama 97-1 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deities={{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}
{{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |Shrine Rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded=(Trad.) {{ill|lt=Yōrō era|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=712|Q23521|qid=Q23521|1=712}}) |Main Hall Style={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative Name= Hokotsuki Kashima Daimyōjin (former name) |Pilgrimage= |Annual Festival={{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|qid=Q2881|1=September 30}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Hokotsuki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hokotsuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Hokotsuki Shrine''' (ほこつきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hokotsuki, {{ill|lt=Sukagawa City|Q819664|qid=Q819664|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} shrine, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} === History === According to tradition, when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} was on his eastern campaign, he climbed Mount Kameiyama (Kamuiyama) and prayed for victory to {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, using a large stone on the mountaintop as an altar. However, the shrine’s origin is said to date to {{ill|lt=Yōrō era|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=712|Q23521|qid=Q23521|1=712}}) in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, when a local clan settled in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Kashima Shrine|Q706499|qid=Q706499|1=Kashima Jingū}}. In {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Kōnin era|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=821|Q30615|qid=Q30615|1=821}}), a sacred spear ({{ill|lt=divine halberd|Q118993698|qid=Q118993698|lt=shin-hoko|1=mao}}) was discovered within the shrine grounds and thereafter enshrined as a {{ill|lt=yorishiro|Q333798|qid=Q333798|1=Yorishiro}} of Kashima Daimyōjin. The shrine is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenki era|Q1193591|qid=Q1193591|1=Tengi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1057|Q19377|qid=Q19377|1=1057}}), during the {{ill|lt=Earlier Nine Years’ War|Q1187448|qid=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Q3082851|qid=Q3082851|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} prayed here for victory and re-enshrined Kashima Shrine together with the existing deity, after which the shrine came to be called Hokotsuki Kashima Daimyōjin. In {{ill|lt=Keichō era|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1597|Q6790|qid=Q6790|1=1597}}), the shrine buildings were destroyed by fire, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Keian era|Q1146135|qid=Q1146135|1=Keian}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1648|Q6941|qid=Q6941|1=1648}}) by {{ill|lt=Sakakibara Tadaji|Q8515268|qid=Q8515268|1=Sakakibara Tadatsugu}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Shirakawa Domain|Q1067555|qid=Q1067555|1=Shirakawa Domain}}. === Shrine Grounds === File:Hokotsuki-jinja, shaden-3.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Hokotsuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (designated cultural property of Fukushima Prefecture) File:Hokotsuki-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Worship Hall (designated cultural property of Sukagawa City) File:Hokotsuki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall (designated cultural property of Sukagawa City) File:Hokotsuki-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Auxiliary shrine File:Hokotsuki-jinja, zuishinmon-2.jpg|Zuishin Gate (designated cultural property of Sukagawa City) File:Hokotsukijinja Site, gaikan.jpg|Hokotsuki Shrine Ritual Site (designated historic site of Sukagawa City) === Festivals === * Taiko Shishi (Drum Lion Dance) – Held once every three years (in leap years of the old lunar calendar) in October: an evening festival on the first Saturday, followed by the main festival on Sunday. Local men from the Hokotsuki area apply makeup, wear costumes, and dance while beating drums. It was designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Sukagawa City on April 1, 1968 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43).[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2982.htm List of Designated Cultural Properties of Sukagawa City] – Sukagawa City, accessed November 12, 2018. === Cultural Properties === === Cultural Properties Designated by Fukushima Prefecture === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Hokotsuki Shrine Main Hall (with 12 ridge tags) (Structure) – Designated May 4, 1976 (Shōwa 51). === Cultural Properties Designated by Sukagawa City === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Hokotsuki Shrine Worship Hall (Structure) – Designated April 1, 1971 (Shōwa 46). ** Hokotsuki Shrine Zuishin Gate (Structure) – Designated April 1, 1971 (Shōwa 46). ** Hokotsuki Shrine Kagura Hall (Structure) – Designated April 1, 1971 (Shōwa 46). ** Hokotsuki Shrine Ema-Tsunagi Koma (Painting) – Stored at the Sukagawa City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 1, 1981 (Shōwa 86). ** Artifacts Excavated from the Hokotsuki Shrine Ritual Site (Archaeological Materials) – Designated March 26, 2001 (Heisei 13). * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Hokotsuki Shrine Taiko Shishi – Designated April 1, 1968 (Shōwa 43). * Historic Sites ** Hokotsuki Shrine Ritual Site – Designated March 17, 1998 (Heisei 10). === Local Information === ;Address * {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sukagawa City|Q819664|qid=Q819664|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}} Hokotsuki, Aza Kameiyama 97-1 ;Access * Bus: {{ill|lt=Fukushima Kōtsū|Q5507741|qid=Q5507741|1=Fukushima Transportation}} (bound for Naganuma via Yadano); get off at “Hokotsuki Shrine” bus stop, then walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} = == See Also === {{Commonscat|Hokotsuki-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Naganuma Castle (Mutsu Province)|Q11653206|qid=Q11653206|1=Naganuma Castle}} * {{ill|lt=Maoi Falls (Sukagawa City)|Q11667948|qid=Q11667948|lt=Maoi Falls|1=Mao Falls}} * {{ill|lt=Iwashiro no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11587159|qid=Q11587159|1=Iwashiro no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hokotsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hokotsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Sukagawa City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sukagawa]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]
